question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookStorageRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdAndKeyBetweenAndDeletedIsFalse()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a fromKey equal to toKey (a zero-width range) to cause the Spring Data JPA query to return all rows matching that single key regardless of the deleted flag, leading to a smart contract hook reading stale deleted storage slots and exhibiting unintended behavior?
